items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has launched the 74th round of its Order Books, Inventories and Capacity Utilisation Survey (OBICUS). The survey is for the reference period April – June 2026 (Q1:2026-27). The Reserve Bank has been conducting the Order Books, Inventories and Capacity Utilisation Survey (OBICUS) of the manufacturing sector on a quarterly basis since 2008. items.title: RBI appoints Shri Ravi Shankar as new Executive Director
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has appointed Shri Ravi Shankar as Executive Director (ED) with effect from July 1, 2026. Prior to his promotion as ED, Shri Shankar was serving as Adviser-In-Charge of the Department of Statistics and Information Management of the Bank. As a career central banker statistician, Shri Shankar has experience of over three decades in the Reserve Bank having worked in the areas of corporate, banking statistics, government securities market and settlement systems, debt management, and surveys in the Reserve Bank. items.title: Tech leads first half stock gains — but the biggest winners weren't in the U.S.
items.summary: U.S. Big Tech stocks enjoyed strong gains — despite a sharp sell-off at the end of June — but were largely outperformed by their international counterparts.

items.title: Private payrolls rose by 98,000 in June, less than expected, ADP reports
items.summary: Companies added slightly fewer workers than forecast, with hiring targeted heavily toward healthcare-related sectors.

items.title: U.S. Treasury yields edge higher as investors await Fed Chair Warsh’s comments
items.summary: U.S. Treasury yields rose on Wednesday, as investors awaited more data and clues on the Federal Reserve's monetary policy path.

items.title: Quote of the day by Edwin Lefevre: "It is not the certainty of disaster ahead but the uncertainty of better days to come that keeps the investor from buying"
items.summary: Financial wisdom highlights that markets often reward those who act amidst uncertainty. Waiting for absolute clarity during downturns can lead to missed opportunities as stock prices recover. History shows strong rallies often begin with weak sentiment. Successful investing hinges on discipline, focusing on long-term fundamentals rather than short-term fears. Uncertainty, therefore, presents a chance to acquire quality assets at good prices for superior returns.

items.title: PE-VC investments down 3% y-o-y in June quarter
items.summary: Mega deals rise but volume falls as early-stage activity shows signs of revival

items.title: Delhi EV buyers get single-window charging push, wider charging network under new policy
items.summary: Delhi's new EV policy aims to boost electric vehicle adoption with a single-window clearance for charging stations and encouragement for housing societies to install chargers. OEMs must set up charging points at dealerships. The government will seek central funding for infrastructure expansion, while DISCOMs explore energy storage and time-of-day tariffs to lower charging costs. A nodal agency will coordinate public charging and battery-swapping efforts.

items.title: Coal India bags Rs 2,831 crore 600 MW solar plant deal in UP
items.summary: State-owned Coal India Limited has clinched a significant ₹2,831.11 crore contract to build a 600 MW solar power facility in Uttar Pradesh's Jalaun Solar Park. This massive project, split into two 300 MW blocks, will supply electricity at a fixed rate of ₹2.73 per kWh. The deal was awarded by Bundelkhand Saur Urja Limited, marking a major green energy push for the mining giant.

items.title: M3M Group to invest Rs 10,000 cr on construction, land acquisition in FY27
items.summary: M3M Group is making a bold move with an investment of Rs 10,000 crore this fiscal year, including Rs 7,200 crore for construction and Rs 2,500 crore for land acquisitions around Gurugram. With a zero-debt status, this developer is looking to enhance its branded housing projects by collaborating with global hospitality and lifestyle brands, while also preparing for upcoming land auctions in Noida to increase its land holdings.

items.title: Demand for riskier mortgages drops, as their advantages shrink
items.summary: The spread between the 30-year fixed-rate mortgage and adjustable-rate loans is narrowing, causing demand for ARMs to weaken.

items.title: Brewery boss who banned phones and swearing from pubs dies aged 81
items.summary: Humphrey Smith had been at the helm of the Tadcaster brewer since the 1980s.

items.title: Nifty snaps losing streak, reclaims 24,000 as Realty and FMCG lead; IT drags
items.summary: Nifty 50 settled at 24,005.85, up 0.59%, while the Sensex advanced 0.58% to close at 76,922

items.title: India bonds stuck in range as traders eye fresh triggers
items.summary: Indian government bonds remained stable as hopes of inclusion in the Bloomberg index balanced out geopolitical concerns and a declining rupee. Traders are on the lookout for new catalysts, with the benchmark 2036 bond yield maintaining a position close to Tuesday's close. The global investment climate stays cautious as U.S. Federal Reserve announcements loom alongside U.S.-Iran negotiations, while domestic markets focus on critical yield levels.

items.title: Dow Jones| Nasdaq | US Stock Market Today | Live: US stocks future slip as US-Iran tensions cloud Middle East peace prospects
Ind-Ra downgrades Jana Capital, Jana Holdings NCDs to default
items.summary: India Ratings downgraded Jana Capital and Jana Holdings' NCDs to default after both entities extended debt repayments by six months due to refinancing challenges. Jana Small Finance Bank shares recovered after an initial decline despite concerns over promoter-level debt.

items.title: Nissan India's June wholesales rise 16%, domestic sales more than double
items.summary: Nissan Motor India witnessed a significant 16% year-on-year jump in total wholesales to 8,346 units last month. Domestic sales surged by an impressive 129%, marking the fourth consecutive month of growth, driven by strong demand for the Magnite and Gravite. This positive momentum fuels confidence as the company gears up for the July 9th debut of its new compact SUV, the Tekton.

items.title: Adani Ports SEZ among 5 stocks hit 52-week highs, rally up to 15% in a month
items.summary: Five BSE 100 stocks, led by Federal Bank and Adani group companies, scaled fresh 52-week highs amid Wednesday's broad-based market rally.

items.title: Buy the UK DIP? Defense stocks lifted by $20 billion spending boost as gilts come under fire
items.summary: British defense stocks are marching higher once again after the U.K. confirmed a near-$20 billion military spending boost on Tuesday.

items.title: Karnataka pitches coach depot for Vijayapura to boost rail connectivity
items.summary: Karnataka Industries Minister MB Patil has proposed a coach depot and an additional entry/exit gate for Vijayapura railway station to enhance rail connectivity. Discussions were held with Union Minister V Somanna, and letters sent to Union Railway Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw. The former goods shed land is earmarked for the depot, aiming to create a maintenance hub and boost train operations for North Karnataka, including the upcoming Bengaluru-Vijayapura Vande Bharat Express.

items.title: Mint Explainer | Who owns the Vadilal brand? A decades-old family dispute surfaces again
items.summary: A decades-old family settlement unravels over allegations of food-quality violations in the latest Vadilal legal battle.

items.title: Trump made more than $1bn from crypto in first year back in office
items.summary: The president's crypto income far outpaces his earnings from real estate and Trump-themed items such as watches.

items.title: New EV Policy: Delhiites to get subsidy in 60 days of owning e-vehicle
items.summary: Delhi's new EV Policy promises subsidies within 60 days for electric vehicle buyers, including two-wheelers up to Rs 2.25 lakh. Incentives for electric two, three, and four-wheeled goods vehicles will be directly transferred. A committee will approve eligible EV models. While electric cars miss purchase incentives, they get road tax and registration fee exemptions until March 2030. The policy details tiered subsidies for two-wheelers and e-autos over three years.

items.title: Retail leasing jumps 18% YoY in Q2 despite supply crunch: Cushman & Wakefield
items.summary: India's retail real estate market is booming, with leasing up significantly quarter-on-quarter and year-on-year. Despite limited new mall supply, retailers are actively leasing space, especially in organized formats and established high streets. This strong demand is leading to tighter vacancies and rising rentals, with fashion and F&B sectors showing particular strength. Future supply additions are expected to ease constraints.

items.title: Kia India sales up 19% at 24,552 units in June
items.summary: Kia India has achieved its highest-ever June sales, reporting a 19% wholesale growth to 24,552 units. The first half of 2026 also marked a record, with 1,63,749 units dispatched, a 15.2% increase year-on-year. This success is attributed to strong demand for models like the Seltos, Sonet, and Carens Clavis, with the new Syros also garnering positive customer reception.

items.title: Microfinance Institutions' AUM projected to grow 20% in FY27: Crisil
items.summary: Microfinance institutions are poised for significant growth, with assets under management expected to surge by 20% in FY27. This acceleration is largely fueled by a strategic shift towards non-microfinance offerings like gold loans and MSME financing, diversifying beyond traditional microcredit. This move aims to bolster resilience and earnings stability amidst inherent risks in the core microfinance segment.

items.title: Up to 150 ex-WHSmith High Street stores to close as rescue deal approved
items.summary: The sweeping restructuring, approved by a court, will see steep rent cuts on most remaining stores.

items.title: The ‘Hi, hello’ monsoon: In rural India, an anxious, high-stakes wait for the skies to swell
items.summary: A teasing sky is chocking central India’s fields and threatening harvests worth millions. Some Basmati growers and tribal farmers now face a brutal choice: wait for the rain, or pack for the factory floor.

items.title: Logistics player A1 expands industrial chemical portfolio
items.summary: A 1 Ltd, a logistics and chemical supplier, has been appointed the Primary Authorised Dealer for Ishan Dyes and Chemicals Ltd's sulphur-based industrial chemicals. This strategic move significantly expands A 1 Ltd's product offerings, including vital chemicals like Sulphuric Acid and Oleum, serving diverse sectors such as fertilisers and pharmaceuticals.

items.title: Japan’s rush to India: The big story behind the Modi-Takaichi summit
items.summary: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi's India visit comes as Japanese investments accelerate across banking, manufacturing, infrastructure and clean energy. Billion-dollar deals, rising corporate interest and closer financial cooperation highlight how Japan is emerging as one of India's most important long-term economic partners, strengthening both investment flows and strategic ties.

items.summary: Brokerages retain positive stance, await clarity on profitability and integration

items.title: Zee Entertainment secures Germany's top-tier football league Bundesliga media rights in India for the next five years
items.summary: Zee Entertainment Enterprises has secured exclusive Indian broadcast and digital rights for Germany's Bundesliga football league for five years, starting 2026-27. Matches will air on Unite8 Sports channels and stream on Zee5, featuring top stars like Harry Kane. This move bolsters Zee's sports portfolio and aims to boost football development in India through initiatives like the 'Z x Bundesliga Football Week'.

items.title: Cordelia Cruises operator Waterways Leisure Tourism shares end 17% below ₹808-IPO price
items.summary: Stock debuts at ₹681 on NSE, a discount of 15.7% to the IPO price of ₹808 per share

items.title: RBI's tighter capital market norms kick in from today; Ashvin Parekh on why the timing matters
items.summary: New Reserve Bank of India regulations, now effective from July 1, aim to bolster financial stability by limiting bank exposure to real estate and securities. These rules, following a deadline extension, restrict the use of third-party collateral and impose explicit caps on lending. Experts suggest the delay allowed for recalibration, ensuring the norms align with market dynamics and prevent excessive leverage during potentially optimistic periods.

items.title: RFBL Flexi Pack bags Rs 20 crore domestic order
items.summary: RFBL Flexi Pack has landed a significant Rs 20 crore order from 3B Films Ltd for transparent and metallized films. This substantial business, to be completed within four months, is set to bolster RFBL's standing in the flexible packaging sector. The company, which reported strong FY26 financials, is also pursuing international expansion with a new UAE subsidiary, underscoring its commitment to diverse industry clients.

items.title: HSBC signs 1.2 million sq ft GCC deal with Prestige Group in Bengaluru
items.summary: HSBC has signed an LOI with Prestige Group for a 1.2 million sq ft, 20-year campus lease in Bengaluru worth over ₹3,000 crore, consolidating its India GCC operations under one roof. The bank will occupy Prestige Lakeshore Drive, with move-in expected next year.

items.title: Royal Enfield sales up 27 pc to 1,14,032 motorcycles in June
items.summary: Royal Enfield revved up sales in June 2026, reporting a significant 27% surge to 1,14,032 motorcycles. This growth is attributed to strong demand for their updated models. Adding to the excitement, the company has begun delivering its groundbreaking electric motorcycle, the Flying Flea C6, in Bengaluru, with plans for a wider rollout. Management expresses optimism for continued sales momentum.

items.title: Decoding BPM 3.0 at the intersection of agility, adaptability, and AI
items.summary: The dawn of BPM 3.0 is here and it is marked by what industry experts term as “process reimagination”. For the three-and-a-half to close to four decades, India has dominated the ITES industry; today, what we are witnessing is an AI-driven shift, where processes are being reimagined with stronger ownership of outcomes, machine-human collaboration, and a more agile approach. Here’s a detailed overview.

items.title: BSE launches REITs and Commercial Real Estate Index for passive investment products
items.summary: BSE Index Services has launched the BSE REITs and Commercial Real Estate Index, India's first benchmark tracking listed REITs and companies with significant commercial real estate exposure. The index is designed to support passive investment products such as ETFs and index funds while offering diversified exposure to yield-generating real estate assets.

items.title: How China’s green tech could boost its global finance ambitions
items.summary: Clean tech leadership will help Beijing’s financial agenda, says former central bank chief economist

items.title: Gold price drops below $4,000, down 12% in June - biggest monthly decline since 2008: what lies ahead?
items.summary: Gold has fallen below the critical $4,000 level, sliding to around $3,960. Analysts predict potential rebounds due to oversold conditions, while long-term fundamentals remain supportive despite ongoing volatility tied to US interest rates and geopolitical factors.

items.title: TikTok plans to cut nearly 300 more jobs at its European hub in Dublin; some at-risk staff may be redeployed
items.summary: TikTok plans to cut approximately 300 jobs at its Dublin hub, following a previous round of layoffs. However, some of the affected staff may be moved to other positions within the firm.

items.summary: Sensex, Nifty, Share Prices Highlights: Market benchmark indices Sensex and Nifty rebounded on Wednesday after two days of decline amid a positive trend in global equities and drop in crude oil prices.

items.title: OpenAI, Anthropic backer MGX raises one of the biggest AI funds ever as it closes at $49 billion
items.summary: The company is a backer of major AI companies including OpenAI and Anthropic, alongside Elon Musk's xAI, prior to its merger with SpaceX.

items.title: Nifty delivered positive returns for 18 of 25 years in July
items.summary: After June gains, historical data suggests July could deliver another positive month for Nifty. The index has risen in 18 of the past 25 Julys, supported by monsoon optimism, earnings expectations, easing crude prices and improving foreign investment flows.

items.title: India corn exports set for three-year high on competitive pricing
items.summary: India's corn exports are soaring to a three-year peak, driven by record production and competitive pricing. Lower domestic prices, coupled with increased freight costs for global competitors, have boosted shipments to Asian nations like Vietnam and Bangladesh. This surge in demand is now helping to stabilize Indian corn prices, with further growth anticipated from October.

items.title: Soybean sowing tops 28.9 lakh hectares, says SOPA
items.summary: Soybean sowing has significantly surpassed official estimates, with the Soybean Processors Association of India reporting 28.923 lakh hectares covered, far exceeding the government's 6.920 lakh hectares. Despite a delayed start due to monsoon patterns, sowing is progressing rapidly in Madhya Pradesh.

items.title: Delhi EV Policy 2026 paves way for hydrogen-powered vehicles
items.summary: Delhi's new Electric Vehicle Policy 2026 paves the way for hydrogen fuel-based vehicles within four years, aiming to curb vehicular emissions, a major contributor to air pollution. The city plans to introduce two hydrogen-powered buses soon, with the Delhi Transport Corporation collaborating with NTPC for this initiative. This move signifies a significant stride towards cleaner, sustainable public transportation and a future-ready mobility ecosystem for the national capital.

items.title: Trump's annual financial disclosure shows more than $580M in crypto-related income
items.summary: Trump's 927-page financial disclosure report for 2025 totals more than 900 pages detailing holdings in cryptocurrency and stocks.

items.title: Top Gainers & losers on 1 July: Reliance Power, Delhivery, Eternal, Paytm, MCX, Dabur India among top gainers
items.summary: The Indian stock market opened positively in July, led by strong buying in realty, FMCG, and auto stocks, with Nifty 50 at 24,005 and Sensex at 76,873. However, IT and metal sectors saw declines as geopolitical tensions impacted crude oil prices.

items.title: JPMorgan names Rahul Badhwar senior country officer for India
items.summary: JPMorgan has appointed Rahul Badhwar as its senior country officer for India, a significant move for the financial giant. Badhwar, formerly the global head of corporate sales at HSBC, will now lead JPMorgan's operations in the country. This strategic appointment underscores the bank's commitment to the Indian market and its growth ambitions. He will be reporting to Sjoerd Leenart, CEO for Asia Pacific.

items.title: Rupee hits near three-week low at 95.24 vs USD as Asian currencies drop on Fed, Iran war caution
items.summary: The Indian rupee hit a near three-week low on Wednesday, mirroring declines in Asian currencies. Investors grew cautious ahead of U.S. Federal Reserve Chair's remarks and uncertainty surrounding U.S.-Iran negotiations. Despite falling oil prices and measures to boost dollar inflows, analysts anticipate no significant rupee recovery in the coming year due to central bank liabilities and subdued foreign portfolio inflows.

items.title: Renfra Energy submits papers with Sebi for IPO; eyes Rs 430-cr via fresh issue
items.summary: Renfra Energy India is gearing up to unveil its Initial Public Offering (IPO), targeting Rs 430 crore by issuing new shares. In addition, the company intends to execute an Offer-for-Sale involving more than 47 lakh shares. The funds will be allocated towards debenture redemption, boosting working capital, and fulfilling general corporate requirements.

items.title: UPI transactions moderate to Rs 28.9 lakh crore in June, up 20% YoY
items.summary: Unified Payments Interface (UPI) transactions saw a slight dip in June, reaching Rs 28.9 lakh crore, down from May's record high. Despite this, the daily average transaction volume hit an all-time peak, highlighting UPI's dominance in everyday digital payments. This growth underscores India's expanding digital payment ecosystem and increasing user trust, with UPI now operational in eight international locations.

items.title: Lamborghini reveals new Urus performance hybrid SUV after ditching EVs
items.summary: Lamborghini is calling the new Urus SE Performante the "fastest Super SUV in the world."

items.title: India's June power consumption rises 11.6% on heatwave, delayed monsoon
items.summary: India's power consumption surged by 11.62% in June, reaching 166.46 billion units, driven by a severe heatwave and delayed monsoon. This pushed demand for cooling appliances, leading to a jump in peak power demand to 264.76 GW. Experts anticipate steady demand in July despite a projected rainfall deficit, following record-breaking peak demand in May.

items.title: 'Rich Dad Poor Dad' Robert Kiyosaki says Indian guru helped him realise making money isn’t life's mission
items.summary: Robert Kiyosaki urged people to pursue their life's purpose instead of chasing money, saying financial success follows meaningful work. Citing an Indian guru and Martin Luther King Jr., he encouraged serving humanity first, arguing that purpose ultimately creates wealth.

items.title: Sensex rises over 400 points, Nifty 50 ends above 24,000; investors earn over ₹2 lakh crore in a day
items.summary: Equity benchmark Sensex clocked a gain of 444 points, or 0.58%, to close at 76,922.64, while the Nifty 50 settled 140 points, or 0.59%, higher at 24,005.85.

items.title: I Squared, TPG-CPPIB consortium nears bid for KKR-backed Re Sustainability
items.summary: I Squared Capital and a TPG-CPPIB consortium are preparing final bids for KKR-backed Re Sustainability in a deal valued at up to $1.4 billion, as rival private equity firms exit the race.

items.title: CBSE asks schools to encourage students, parents to take cyber hygiene certification course
items.summary: Schools affiliated with CBSE are now mandated to champion cyber safety. This initiative, part of a national campaign, encourages students, teachers, and parents to complete a cyber hygiene course and form cyber clubs. Monthly awareness activities, including quizzes and pledge drives, will be organized to combat online risks like grooming and cyberbullying, fostering a safer digital environment for all.

items.title: HMSI posts 23% rise in sales to 5,28,281 units in June
items.summary: Honda Motorcycle & Scooter India reported a robust 23% surge in total sales for June, reaching 5,28,281 units. Domestic sales climbed 21% to 4.68 lakh units, while exports saw an impressive 47% jump. The first quarter of the fiscal year also witnessed a healthy 17% growth in total vehicle sales.

items.title: India imported record crude volumes in June despite West Asia tensions: Kpler
items.summary: The import volumes underscore India's ability to maintain crude supplies through diversified sourcing even as conflict in West Asia raised concerns over global energy shipments & briefly pushed up oil prices

items.title: How government’s ₹18,560-crore stake sales lit up a lacklustre stock market
items.summary: Government trims its holdings in six companies in the three months through June, resulting in its largest quarterly haul in recent years

items.title: Getty scraps $3.7bn Shutterstock merger after UK watchdog order
items.summary: US regulators had already cleared combination of rivals when CMA imposed conditions

items.title: European markets ride oil tailwinds, but can they challenge Wall Street’s tech supremacy?
items.summary: European equities are rallying as lower crude prices following the Iran ceasefire improve the region’s near-term outlook, easing inflation and boosting corporate margins. While the STOXX 600 has hit record highs, investors remain unconvinced that the gains will trigger a lasting shift away from Wall Street, where AI-driven earnings growth continues to underpin stronger market performance.

items.title: Union Cabinet approves six-lane Dwarka tunnel, Kanpur-Kabrai highway worth Rs 14,115 crore
items.summary: The Union Cabinet has greenlit two significant highway projects totaling over Rs 14,000 crore. A six-lane tunnel will soon connect Dwarka Expressway to South Delhi, easing congestion and boosting urban mobility. Additionally, a new greenfield highway will link Kanpur and Kabrai in Uttar Pradesh, promising faster travel and improved logistics for regional economic growth.

items.title: Nifty eyes 25,000 as July rally builds; banking, realty and auto lead the charge: Rohit Srivastava
items.summary: Market strategist Rohit Srivastava anticipates a strong summer for Indian equities, with the Nifty poised for gains. He highlights banking, realty, and auto sectors as key beneficiaries of favorable seasonal trends and interest rate sensitivity. While pharma shows long-term promise, IT stocks are advised to be avoided. FMCG offers a short-term tactical opportunity.

items.title: Who is Rajiv Kumar? The ex-poll panel chief who oversaw India's largest elections and is now set to lead HDFC Bank
items.summary: HDFC Bank appoints Rajiv Kumar as part-time Chairman for four years following Atanu Chakraborty's resignation. Kumar, a former CEC, is recognized for his banking reforms and overseeing the 2024 Lok Sabha elections. Details here.

items.title: Coal India production drops 7.5 pc in Q1
items.summary: Coal India Limited (CIL) reported a 7.5% dip in production to 169.6 million tonnes during the first quarter of the current fiscal year, despite soaring power sector demand. This comes as India gears up for increased power demand, highlighting CIL's crucial role in national energy security.

items.title: Japanese Yen sinks to weakest level since 1986: 3 reasons behind the historic slide

items.title: Tata Motors Passenger Vehicles' June sales jump 69%; EV volumes more than double

Domestic passenger vehicle sales also saw a significant 67% jump. The company's leadership in electric mobility continues to strengthen, with EV volumes more than doubling year-on-year. Tata Motors remains optimistic about sustaining growth, citing a healthy order book and positive customer response.

items.title: HSBC inks pact with Prestige Group to pre-lease 1.2 million sq ft office in Bengaluru

This significant deal, valued at over Rs 3,000 crore for a 20-year lease, highlights Bengaluru's appeal for multinational financial firms. The under-construction campus, set for completion in October 2027, underscores the strong demand for integrated, high-quality office spaces in India's tech and finance hub.

items.title: Anupam Misra takes charge as CMD of Hindustan Copper
items.summary: Hindustan Copper Ltd has appointed Anupam Misra as its new Chairman & Managing Director, taking over from Sanjiv Kumar Singh. items.title: Infosys shares slip below ₹1,000 for first time since 2020 as IT rout deepens; m-cap falls to ₹4 lakh crore

items.title: Volvo Eicher Commercial Vehicles sales up 29.3% at 9,519 units in June
items.summary: VE Commercial Vehicles, a joint venture between Volvo Group and Eicher Motors, reported a significant 29.3% surge in total sales for June 2026, reaching 9,519 units. items.title: Sunil Bharti Mittal hopeful of India-US trade deal in coming weeks

Prem Watsa, and Christopher T. Calio for their contributions to the India-US economic partnership.

items.title: Nearly half of Indian households need electrical upgrades for EV charging: Report

The research highlights overlooked challenges in residential charging, from inadequate wiring to parking issues, underscoring the need for robust electrical infrastructure alongside policy support for a successful electric mobility transition.

This technology, now proven, holds immense promise for future vaccines and personalized treatments, though equitable global access remains crucial.

items.summary: The Indian government is creating a standardized methodology to estimate the economic output of cities, known as City Economic Product. items.summary: India's provident fund system has undergone a significant overhaul with the introduction of the Employees' Provident Fund Scheme, 2026, replacing the 1952 version. items.title: In Graphics: How GST cuts triggered a rally for Indian businesses
items.summary: Government analysis reveals that last September's Goods and Services Tax rate adjustments led to a significant expansion of the tax base. Higher taxable supplies more than compensated for reduced tax rates, boosting average monthly taxable supply by 22.5% in the latter half of FY26. This positive outcome marks a notable impact as the GST nears its ninth anniversary.

items.title: Fiscal deficit widens to 9.6% of FY27 target on higher spending
items.summary: India's fiscal deficit surged to 9.6% of the annual target in the first two months of the financial year, a significant jump from last year's 0.8%. This was largely due to increased spending in April, though May saw a surplus of ₹2 lakh crore, aided by a record dividend from the central bank. Despite higher subsidy and capital expenditure, analysts believe the government can still meet its FY27 deficit target.

items.title: Easing oil prices, supply chains to soften external pressures on India: Finance ministry
items.summary: India's economy remains resilient despite global uncertainties, with easing oil prices and improving supply chains offering some relief. However, monsoon vagaries and West Asian geopolitical shifts pose risks to growth and inflation. Foreign investors are returning to sovereign debt, signaling confidence. The finance ministry emphasizes building national buffer stocks and reorienting farm policies towards climate-resilient crops, highlighting the need for proactive policy measures.

items.title: India's manufacturing growth slows in Q1 as West Asia conflict weighs: FICCI
items.summary: India's manufacturing sector anticipates a slower growth phase in the April-June quarter of FY27, influenced by the West Asia conflict. While production and order books saw a dip, production costs, driven by raw materials and energy, continued to climb. Despite these challenges, export sentiment showed a positive uptick, suggesting diversification efforts are bearing fruit.

items.title: New macro indicators in the works to track Viksit Bharat progress
items.summary: India is crafting a robust Viksit Bharat Sankalp indicator framework, featuring around 385 macro indicators, to meticulously track its journey towards becoming a developed nation by 2047. This data-driven mechanism, spearheaded by the Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation, will assess economic, social, and infrastructure progress across key sectors. The comprehensive system aims for implementation by 2031, ensuring inclusive and sustainable development is at the forefront.

items.title: Clouds over kharif sowing: Met sees below-normal rain in July
items.summary: July brings a worrying forecast for India as monsoon rains are predicted to be below normal, falling short of 94% of the long-term average. This situation could adversely affect the kharif sowing season critical for crops such as paddy and pulses.

items.title: Automobiles biggest driver of India's export growth to Japan: Report
items.summary: India's automotive exports to Japan have surged, becoming the primary growth engine and now representing 13% of shipments by FY26, up from 1% in FY21. This highlights deeper integration into Japan's auto supply chains. Meanwhile, Japanese firms continue to view India as a top investment hub, with most planning expansions. Despite rising exports, India's trade deficit with Japan has widened significantly due to faster import growth.

items.title: IBBI seeks to streamline real estate insolvency regulations with focus on homebuyers
items.summary: India's bankruptcy watchdog is proposing significant reforms to expedite real estate insolvency. Key changes include excluding completed or unaffected housing projects with creditor approval and mandating project-wise cash flow management. The aim is to prioritize project completion, enhance transparency for homebuyers, and ensure accountability, with stakeholders invited to provide feedback by July 21.

items.title: PM Modi interacts with govt secretaries; discusses reforms for ease of doing business, ease of living

Modi emphasized integrated planning and a whole-of-government approach, urging secretaries to ensure tangible benefits for citizens through effective governance and service delivery.

items.title: Corporate debt-servicing capacity improves, but share of vulnerable firms rises in Q4 FY26: RBI
items.summary: Indian companies are showing improved debt-servicing capabilities, with the interest coverage ratio reaching a two-year high of 6.5 in March 2026. This indicates a stronger ability to meet interest payments from operating profits. However, a concerning rise in financially vulnerable firms, whose profits can't cover interest, was also noted. Despite this, the overall debt burden remains manageable, and corporate leverage is declining.

items.title: Govt approves proposal to amend Khadi and Village Industries Commission Act
items.summary: The government has approved amendments to the KVIC Act, 1956, to modernize the Khadi and Village Industries sector. These changes aim to boost rural enterprises, enhance competitiveness through branding and innovation, and broaden the definition of rural areas. The reforms will also improve governance with greater representation and empower the government to notify new village industries, fostering inclusive economic growth and employment.

items.title: Economy resilient but risks from monsoon, geopolitical uncertainties remain: FinMin report
items.summary: India's economy shows resilience despite a slight slowdown in some key indicators. While global commodity prices ease, risks loom from an uneven monsoon, potential El Nino, and geopolitical tensions. Policymakers are focusing on enhancing resilience to energy shocks and addressing water scarcity, with agricultural pricing policies also under review to promote climate-resilient crops. Foreign investment is showing positive signs.

items.title: RBI flags emerging fault lines in private credit market, calls for close monitoring

The central bank's latest report highlights an uptick in defaults and investor redemption requests, signaling emerging stress. While not currently a systemic threat, the RBI emphasizes the critical need for ongoing monitoring to safeguard the broader financial system from potential risks.

items.title: Household sector debt climbs to 45.5 pc of GDP in Sep 2025: RBI report

Despite this rise, borrower quality is improving, with more prime borrowers taking out loans. While consumption loans dominate, experts express concern over debt servicing for depreciating assets. India ranks fourth among emerging economies in household debt as a percentage of GDP.

While recent policy measures have eased pressure on the rupee and bond yields, attracting capital remains a focus amidst global uncertainties.

items.title: Bank credit to industry grows at robust 17.5% in May: RBI

A large portion of these loans are unsecured and target younger borrowers, signaling increased risk in this fast-growing segment.
